projects.title: Student Attendance Management System
projects.meta: Web Application · Academic Project · ASP.NET Core MVC
projects.description: A full-featured web-based Student Attendance Management System with three roles: Admins, Lecturers, and Students. Each gets a tailored dashboard — from user management to session marking to attendance history.
projects.why: Manual attendance at UICT wastes lecture time, creates errors, and leaves students with no real-time insight into their records. This system digitalises the entire flow.
projects.problem: Lecturers called names from printed lists. Students had no way to verify their standing, leading to disputes. Administrators had no unified reporting system.
projects.solution: Built a multi-role MVC system: Lecturers mark attendance per session, Admins manage users and generate reports, Students view their own history in real time. All data flows through MySQL with EF Core migrations.

projects.title: LAB 112 UICT Student Portal
projects.meta: Progressive Web App · Open Source · GitHub Pages
projects.description: A Progressive Web App student portal for the LAB 112 class at UICT Nakawa. Fully installable on any device, works offline, and centralises everything students need: PDF notes, C# resources, a deadline calendar, and class tools.
projects.why: Students had no organised digital hub. Notes lived in WhatsApp forwards, deadlines got missed, and nothing was accessible without internet.
projects.problem: Resources scattered across platforms. No offline access. No unified calendar. No lightweight installable solution built specifically for the class.
projects.solution: Deployed a fully offline-capable PWA with PDF viewer, C# notes library, interactive deadline calendar, and PWA installability. Cache versioning (CACHE_NAME: lab112-v3) ensures fresh content on every update.

projects.title: Expense Tracker App
projects.description: A client-side expense tracker — log income and expenses, categorise transactions, view running totals, and persist data between sessions. No backend required.
projects.why: Built to solidify core JavaScript skills: DOM manipulation, event-driven architecture, and persistent client-side storage.
projects.problem: Most expense apps require accounts and internet. A simple, private, offline-first tracker that lives in the browser needed no database at all.
projects.solution: Stored all transactions in localStorage as serialized JSON. Dynamic DOM updates, category-based filtering, and a real-time running balance — all pure vanilla JavaScript.

projects.title: JavaScript Quiz App
projects.description: An interactive multiple-choice quiz with timed questions, live progress tracking, score calculation, and a results screen — all dynamically rendered.
projects.why: The goal: master JavaScript control flow, dynamic DOM generation, and stateful UI rendering without any framework.
projects.problem: Needed a real challenge in programmatic DOM management — building, destroying, and updating UI elements entirely from JavaScript state.
projects.solution: Built a stateful quiz engine that shuffles questions on each run, renders options programmatically, tracks answers, shows a live progress bar, and presents a detailed per-question results breakdown.

projects.title: UIAHMS Ministry Event Flyer
projects.meta: UI Design · Guild Work · Code-Only Design
projects.description: A fully code-rendered promotional flyer for the UIAHMS Ministry of Justice and Constitutional Affairs (27th Guild at UICT) — designed with zero design tools, entirely in HTML, CSS, and SVG.
projects.why: As a member of the Ministry of Justice in the student guild, I was asked to create event material. Without Photoshop or Canva, I proved it could be done in code.
projects.problem: No access to graphic design software. Needed a print-quality, institutionally-branded flyer with structured layout and visual hierarchy — fast.
projects.solution: Leveraged layered CSS, custom SVG icons, typographic hierarchy, and absolute positioning to produce a visually polished flyer printable directly from the browser.
